title: Frequency Maps, Counter Semantics, and Reconciliation · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in inspectable frequency engine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Build frequencies with explicit dictionary or Counter updates and explain missing-key and zero-count semantics.
Return a fresh result or an explicitly owned mutable result and prevent hidden cross-run state.
Assert that nonnegative frequencies sum to the accepted token count and preserve excluded-token evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Count Complexity
steps.instruction:
Order the Count Complexity Bookbot and Python corpus-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Count Complexity.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Count Complexity assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Count Complexity, keep Count Complexity connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Bookbot team handles case 5689 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce an owned frequency map whose nonnegative counts reconcile to accepted tokens; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive expected time and space cost from tokens and vocabulary before measuring implementation behavior.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Count Complexity
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-count-complexity-1uqu21" that uses case 4952 to produce an owned frequency map whose nonnegative counts reconcile to accepted tokens. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-count-complexity-1uqu21
# Competency: bookbot-count-complexity.
# Case 4952: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_count_complexity_1uqu21(token_count: int = 12, frequency_total: int = 12, vocabulary: int = 5, negative_counts: int = 0, owned_result: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1uqu21 = "1uqu21-12uqu1-4952"
    assert evidence_variant_1uqu21
    evidence_axes_1uqu21 = (
        "corpus1uqu21",
        "policy12uqu1",
        "revision4952",
        "command1uqu214952",
        "report12uqu14952",
        "test49521uqu21",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1uqu21)) == 6
    if token_count < 0 or vocabulary < 0:
        return False, token_count, "count-shape"
    if negative_counts > 0:
        return False, negative_counts, "negative-frequency"
    if frequency_total != token_count:
        return False, frequency_total, "reconciliation"
    if not owned_result:
        return False, vocabulary, "aliased-result"
    assert vocabulary <= token_count
    return True, vocabulary, "reconciled-counter"
